meta.description: Industry discovery via Wikipedia link walking
meta.method: Pull Wikipedia links, pick physical inputs, follow the tree

semiconductor_equipment.name: Semiconductor Equipment
semiconductor_equipment.wikipedia: Semiconductor equipment sales leaders by year

neodymium_magnet.name: Neodymium Magnet
neodymium_magnet.note: Leads to rare earth mining — geopolitically critical

solid_state_drive.name: Solid-State Drive
solid_state_drive.note: Substitute for HDD — same industry by substitution test

neodymium.name: Neodymium
neodymium.note: Rare earth element, China dominates supply

dysprosium.name: Dysprosium
dysprosium.note: Critical rare earth for high-temp magnets

electrical_steel.name: Electrical Steel
electrical_steel.note: Silicon steel laminations — every motor and transformer needs this

electric_vehicle.name: Electric Vehicle
electric_vehicle.note: Demand node — connects back to automotive industry in existing graph

cover_glass.name: Cover Glass (Gorilla Glass etc.)
cover_glass.note: Specialty chemically strengthened glass, Corning dominates

organoiridium.name: Organoiridium Compound
organoiridium.note: Phosphorescent OLED emitters, key IP held by Universal Display Corp

indium_tin_oxide.name: Indium Tin Oxide (ITO)
indium_tin_oxide.note: Transparent conductor, critical — indium is scarce

catalytic_converter.name: Catalytic Converter
catalytic_converter.note: Needs platinum, palladium, rhodium — precious metals

vehicle_glass.name: Vehicle Glass (Windshield)
vehicle_glass.note: Laminated safety glass — float glass + PVB interlayer

engine_control_unit.name: Engine Control Unit (ECU)
engine_control_unit.note: Automotive semiconductor — biggest auto chip demand

automotive_battery.name: Automotive Lead-Acid Battery
automotive_battery.note: 12V lead-acid, not EV battery (which is lithium-ion)

lidar.name: LiDAR
lidar.note: Autonomous driving sensor, also used in surveying/mapping

rhodium.name: Rhodium
rhodium.note: Catalytic converters, extremely rare and expensive

silver.name: Silver
silver.note: Solar cell contacts — solar is now largest silver consumer globally

tellurium.name: Tellurium
tellurium.note: Very rare, byproduct of copper refining

gallium.name: Gallium
gallium.note: GaAs solar + LEDs + 5G chips. China controls 80%+
